records.author: Kate Magill
records.title: Schneider Electric to invest $700M in US manufacturing
records.body: Schneider Electric plans to invest $700 million in its U.S. operations through 2027. The money will fund facility upgrades, expansions and openings across eight sites to meet rising demand from data centers and electricity infrastructure.

records.author: Nathan Owens
records.title: Jabil picks North Carolina for $500M AI facility
records.body: Jabil plans to spend $500 million to expand its U.S. manufacturing footprint in the Southeast to support surging data center demand. The project is expected to create nearly 1,200 jobs and have the facility operational by mid-2026.

records.author: Nathan Owens
records.title: ABB to invest $120M in US manufacturing
records.body: ABB said it will invest $120 million to expand production of low-voltage electrification products in the U.S. The investment will increase capacity at facilities in Tennessee and Mississippi to support growing demand from data centers, buildings and utilities.

records.author: Joelle Anselmo
records.title: GE Vernova to invest nearly $600M in US factories
records.body: GE Vernova will spend nearly $600 million on U.S. factories and facilities over two years. The investment will create over 1,500 jobs and focus on gas power, grid, nuclear and onshore wind manufacturing sites.

records.author: Nathan Owens
records.title: Power enclosure maker AVL to establish its first US plant
records.body: AVL Manufacturing will invest $56 million to establish its first U.S. production facility in North Carolina. The plant will make industrial enclosures for large-scale power generators used in data centers and create about 300 jobs.

records.author: Nathan Owens
records.title: Rolls-Royce invests $75M in South Carolina engine plant
records.body: Rolls-Royce will invest $75 million to expand its Aiken, South Carolina, engine plant to meet surging data center demand. The 60,000-square-foot expansion will increase production of large engines used to power data centers and industrial facilities.

records.author: Nathan Owens
records.title: Rockwell Automation confirms Wisconsin factory location, part of $2B US expansion
records.body: Rockwell Automation plans to build a new factory in Wisconsin as part of a $2 billion effort to strengthen its U.S. operations. The campus will include more than 1 million square feet of manufacturing and warehouse space.

records.author: Eric Walz
records.title: Hyundai boosts US investments to $26B through 2028
records.body: Hyundai increased its U.S. investment commitment to $26 billion from 2025 to 2028 to expand vehicle production capacity, fund a Louisiana steel plant and build a robotics facility with annual capacity of 30,000 units.

records.author: Joelle Anselmo
records.title: Mitsubishi Electric subsidiary invests $86M in switchgear factory
records.body: Mitsubishi Electric Power Products will invest $86 million in a 160,000-square-foot switchgear factory near Pittsburgh and upgrade existing facilities. The move responds to growing demand for transmission, distribution and data center power products.

records.author: Elise Reuter
records.title: Roche to expand, open Indianapolis and North Carolina sites
records.body: Roche Diagnostics will build a $550 million expansion at an Indianapolis facility to produce continuous glucose monitors and a new $700 million factory in Holly Springs, North Carolina.

records.author: Dan Swinhoe
records.title: Rowan Digital breaks ground on 300MW data center campus outside San Antonio, Texas
records.body: Rowan Digital Infrastructure has broken ground on a 300MW hyperscale campus outside San Antonio. The $900 million project is tailored to a top five U.S. technology company, with an initial 60MW expected in early 2026.

records.author: Dan Swinhoe
records.title: CyrusOne breaks ground on Fort Worth data center campus in Texas
records.body: CyrusOne broke ground on a new multi-building campus in Fort Worth, Texas. The project will deliver an initial IT capacity of around 70MW, with total capital investments in Texas exceeding $2.4 billion.

records.author: Jason Ma
records.title: EdgeCore expands Mesa data center campus in Arizona
records.body: EdgeCore is expanding its Mesa campus by adding nearly 44 acres and lifting total capacity from more than 200MW to more than 450MW to meet urgent hyperscale demand for AI and cloud-ready capacity.

records.author: Niva Yadav
records.title: EdgeCore begins construction at 114MW Northern Virginia campus
records.body: EdgeCore began construction at a 114MW campus in Northern Virginia with delivery expected in 2026. The company is also topping out additional facilities on its 450MW campus in Mesa, Arizona.

records.author: Dan Swinhoe
records.title: EdgeCore secures $235 million in ABS financing
records.body: EdgeCore completed a $235 million asset-backed securitization backed by its fully leased Phoenix-area data center. Proceeds will refinance construction loans and fund future green data center development.

records.author: Dan Swinhoe
records.title: Rowan secures $500m for 300MW Texas data center project
records.body: Rowan Digital Infrastructure secured approximately $550 million of construction financing for the first development phase of its 300MW Cinco campus outside San Antonio.

records.author: Zachary Skidmore
records.title: PG&E data center pipeline swells to 10GW
records.body: PG&E reported 10GW of data center capacity in its pipeline, with 17 projects totaling 1.5GW in final engineering phases and operations expected to begin between 2026 and 2030.

records.author: Zachary Skidmore
records.title: US utility Exelon reports data center pipeline of 33GW
records.body: Exelon said it has 33GW of data centers interested in connecting to its system, with 17GW already in line to connect. The utility is considering options for building and owning generation to meet the demand.

records.author: Zachary Skidmore
records.title: First Energy data center pipeline surges to 2.6GW by 2029
records.body: First Energy reported that its pipeline of potential data centers surged to 2.6GW by 2029, almost double the prior reported pipeline. The utility is considering delaying coal retirements or adding gas-fired generation to serve the load.

records.author: Zachary Skidmore
records.title: PPL Electric's data center pipeline soars to 14GW
records.body: PPL Electric said its advanced-stage data center interconnection pipeline grew to 14GW. The utility is taking a two-pronged approach to support data center growth and expects load to ramp sharply over the decade.

records.author: Diana DiGangi
records.title: Eaton invests $340M in US transformer production
records.body: Eaton will invest $340 million to increase U.S. production of three-phase transformers in response to an ongoing shortage and record demand from utilities, industrial customers and data centers.

records.author: Robert Walton
records.title: Hitachi Energy commits $250M to address transformer shortage
records.body: Hitachi Energy announced a $250 million investment to expand global production of transformer components, with more than 40% dedicated to the U.S. The company said data center development has pushed transformer demand beyond initial projections.

records.author: Kate Magill
records.title: Schneider Electric to invest $700M in US manufacturing
records.body: Schneider Electric plans to invest $700 million through 2027 for facility upgrades, expansions and openings across eight U.S. sites to meet rising demand across data centers, utilities and energy infrastructure.

records.author: Robert Walton
records.title: DTE inks first data center deal to grow electric load 25%
records.body: DTE signed a 1.4GW agreement to serve a hyperscale data center and sees another 7GW of potential large loads. The utility now has a $30 billion investment pipeline for generation, distribution and other infrastructure from 2026 to 2030.

records.author: Ethan Howland
records.title: Exelon data center pipeline jumps to 17 GW as load forecast turns positive
records.body: Exelon utilities' pipeline of data centers and other high-density load projects more than doubled to 17GW. The company expects positive load growth and called for new approaches to ensure adequate power supplies.

records.author: Ethan Howland
records.title: FirstEnergy’s 5-year data center pipeline doubles to 3 GW
records.body: FirstEnergy's pipeline of potential data centers nearly doubled to almost 3GW by 2029. The utility expects industrial sales including data centers to grow 5.1% annually over the next five years.

records.author: Robert Walton
records.title: Hitachi unveils $1B grid manufacturing investment, including Virginia transformer factory
records.body: Hitachi Energy will build a $457 million transformer production facility in South Boston, Virginia, and make more than $500 million in other investments to meet growing need for U.S. grid equipment amid transformer bottlenecks.

records.author: Diana DiGangi
records.title: As load grows, Southern raises spending plan to $81B
records.body: Southern Company raised its five-year spending plan to $81 billion, driven by substantial new growth infrastructure investments as large-load and data center demand increases. The company is working on adding 10GW of approved new generation.

records.author: Diana DiGangi
records.title: FirstEnergy expects peak load to grow 45% by 2035 on data centers
records.body: FirstEnergy expects system peak load to jump 45% by 2035, driven by data center development. The company said developers will be responsible for incremental investments needed to support that growth.

records.author: Joelle Anselmo
records.title: Italy-based Westrafo to build its first US transformer plant
records.body: Westrafo will invest at least $12 million to establish its first U.S. transformer plant in Ohio, with production expected to start in 2025 and output of 12GW per year by 2027.
